paper_title: Western blot analysis and Preliminary study on PP in BPH therapy

methods.description: Western blot analysis was performed to detect specific proteins in a sample using antibodies. This method is used to analyze protein expression levels and post-translational modifications.

steps.description: Each sample was mixed with a lysis buffer containing protease and phosphatase inhibitors followed by trituration and centrifugation.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare protein samples by lysis, trituration, and centrifugation

steps.description: The protein concentration was measured using BCA protein assay.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure protein concentration using BCA assay

steps.description: 30 μg of protein samples were separated by 9 % SDS-PAGE gel and blotted onto PVDF membranes.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate proteins by SDS-PAGE and transfer to PVDF membranes

steps.description: Membranes were blocked with 5 % skim milk dissolved in PBST for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Block membranes with skim milk in PBST

steps.description: Incubate with the primary antibody diluted in PBST at 4°C overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate membrane with primary antibody overnight

steps.description: The membranes were washed in PBST and incubated with appropriate horseradish peroxidase-conjugated secondary antibodies.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash membrane and incubate with secondary antibody

steps.description: Immunoreactive bands were visualized by ECL.
Band intensities were measured with a Tanon 5200 imager and quantified by Image J.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify band intensities using Image J

steps.description: The proteins were normalized against GAPDH.

methods.method_name: Preliminary Study on PP in BPH Therapy
methods.description: This study investigates the substance composition and mechanism of PP in BPH therapy, focusing on identifying active ingredients and their targets, and analyzing their interactions and pathways.

steps.description: From the literature review, 196 chemical constituents were identified in PP.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify chemical constituents of PP from literature

steps.description: Among these chemical constituents, 81 active ingredients had good bioavailability and gastrointestinal absorption.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze bioavailability and absorption of active ingredients

steps.description: 914 and 1031 active ingredients and targets for BPH were collected from the above drug databases, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect active ingredients and targets for BPH from databases

steps.description: 177 overlapping targets were obtained, and protein interaction analysis was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform protein interaction analysis on overlapping targets

steps.description: GO and KEGG analysis were performed to build the 'drug-compound-target-pathway-disease' network.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct GO and KEGG analysis to build interaction network

steps.description: KEGG pathway enrichment analysis diagram shows that the PI3K/Akt pathway has the highest number of enriched differential genes.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze KEGG pathway enrichment for PI3K/Akt pathway
